description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 AI-platform-ops cluster: model-routing/gateway layer vs cost policy vs telemetry implementation vs credential custody vs output schema, and against the shipped observability-operator and secrets-identity-hardener.

cases.prompt: Design the gateway all our model calls go through — provider selection, fallback when one is down, and one place to hold the keys.
cases.reason: The routing/gateway layer (selection, fallback, custody, choke point) is this skill; ai-cost-guardrail-designer sets the budget policy it enforces and observability-operator implements the telemetry it emits.

cases.prompt: Set the token caps, per-tenant budgets, and rate limits for our AI feature and defend against token-drain.
cases.reason: The consumption/cost policy is ai-cost-guardrail-designer; the router enforces those limits but doesn't decide them.

cases.prompt: Implement the dashboards and alerts for our AI call latency, error rate, and cost.
cases.reason: Hands-on dashboard/alert implementation is observability-operator; the router defines the per-call telemetry contract and emits it, but doesn't build the dashboards.

cases.prompt: Our provider API key leaked into the client bundle via a VITE_ variable — move it server-side and rotate it.
cases.reason: Moving/rotating a leaked secret with a bundle-absence proof is secrets-identity-hardener; the router composes it for custody but the remediation itself is that skill's.

cases.prompt: Define and enforce the JSON schema the model must return so downstream code can rely on the shape.
cases.reason: Output shape/schema validation is structured-output-validator; the router moves requests/responses but doesn't own the output contract.
